text.title: Giving and Receiving Feedback Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Feedback reads 'you're not detail-oriented.' Recipient hears identity attack.
antipattern.fix: Replace with observable behavior + impact: 'In the launch checklist last Tuesday, 4 of 10 items were unchecked at sign-off; the launch slipped 2h.'

antipattern.symptom: Author delivers feedback in front of team chat; recipient defensive forever.
antipattern.fix: Move to private channel; feedback in public is humiliation regardless of content.

antipattern.symptom: Author wants to be kind, omits the impact, recipient learns nothing.
antipattern.root-cause: Care axis high, challenge axis low.
antipattern.fix: Tag honestly; if quadrant is ruinous-empathy, rewrite with explicit impact line.

antipattern.symptom: Receiving feedback, author cuts in with 'but actually...' — feedback loop closes.
antipattern.fix: Force a 5s pause before responding; use 'thank you, I want to think about this' if needed.
